items.title: ISR / on-demand revalidation
items.detection_method: prerender-manifest.json shows /blog/[slug] prerendered with initialRevalidateSeconds: 3600 (2 paths); no revalidateTag/revalidatePath calls found in the available source configs
items.weight_rationale: Portable via OpenNext but requires the tag cache and revalidation queue to be provisioned as a unit, not independently - see Pre-Flight Check I1.
items.upgrade_input: upgrade to Next.js >= 16.2 for the Adapter API's typed build output (a confidence upgrade offer, never a migration prerequisite)

items.title: Edge middleware
items.detection_method: middleware.ts exists (prescan has_middleware: true); matcher covers /dashboard/:path* (auth_gating) and /old-blog/:path* (rewrite)
items.weight_rationale: Behavioral divergence on every AWS outcome - see Pre-Flight Check M1 (flagship).
items.upgrade_input: upgrade to Next.js >= 16.2 for the Adapter API's typed build output (a confidence upgrade offer, never a migration prerequisite)

items.title: Edge runtime routes
items.detection_method: no runtime: 'edge' declarations found in next.config.js or available route segment configs
items.weight_rationale: Edge runtime routes hit Lambda@Edge-style constraints (smaller runtime, limited Node API surface) when migrated.
items.upgrade_input: full app/ source tree access (only build manifests and top-level configs are present in the workspace)

items.title: Image optimization
items.detection_method: next.config.js images.remotePatterns configured for cdn.acme-shop.com; next/image component usage not directly verifiable (app/ source tree not present in workspace)
items.weight_rationale: Portable - OpenNext ships its own sharp-based image optimization; see Pre-Flight Check B3 for the duplicate-dependency conflict.
items.upgrade_input: full app/ source tree access (only build manifests and top-level configs are present in the workspace)

items.title: Streaming SSR
items.detection_method: no streaming response patterns (ReadableStream, Response with stream body) found; route handler source files not present in workspace to scan
items.weight_rationale: A known Lambda workaround exists for empty-body streams (see Pre-Flight Check S1); next start (Outcome B) streams natively.
items.upgrade_input: full app/ source tree access (only build manifests and top-level configs are present in the workspace)

items.title: Server Actions / version skew exposure
items.detection_method: Next.js 15.1.0 (App Router in use per app-path-routes-manifest.json) supports Server Actions, but Server Actions usage in source cannot be verified - app/ source tree not present in workspace
items.weight_rationale: Vercel handles skew protection (mismatched client/server deploy versions) automatically; the AWS path must address this explicitly regardless of outcome.
items.upgrade_input: full app/ source tree access (only build manifests and top-level configs are present in the workspace)

items.title: Preview deployments
items.detection_method: Clarify Q4 (team workflow) - not detectable from code alone; note: the deployments API capture shows 2 recent preview deployments, suggesting active use, but the resolved value comes from clarify-answers.json
items.weight_rationale: No AWS equivalent exists; drives the Outcome C (Hybrid) precedence rule. The single heaviest 'what you lose' item in every report.

items.title: Vercel KV / Postgres / Blob / Edge Config / Cron
items.detection_method: Vercel API stores capture: KV store 'acme-cart-cache' (50 MB, iad1) and Postgres store 'acme-orders-db' (3 GB, iad1); one cron in vercel.json; @vercel/kv and @vercel/postgres are package.json dependencies
items.weight_rationale: Clean peripheral mappings exist for all of these - see knowledge/peripheral-mappings.json.
items.upgrade_input: infrastructure-pointing env var hostnames shared explicitly by the founder (Tier 3)

items.title: Vercel-injected headers (geo, IP)
items.detection_method: grep for x-vercel-ip-* and similar headers across middleware.ts and available server code found no usage
items.weight_rationale: Needs a CloudFront-headers rewrite mapping - see Pre-Flight Check M2.
items.upgrade_input: full app/ source tree access (only build manifests and top-level configs are present in the workspace)

phased_migration_candidate.note: coupling is spread across ISR, middleware, and managed stores at ordinary levels - no single component is at unusually high coupling relative to the rest
